directives.title: Minimal viable DB connection with health check endpoint

Then create a GET handler at `app/api/health/db/route.ts` that executes `SELECT 1` and returns `{ status: 'ok', latency_ms: number }` on success or `{ status: 'error', message: string }` with HTTP 503 on failure. Include a Jest test at `__tests__/lib/db/connection.test.ts` that mocks the Neon client and asserts both success and failure paths. No schema creation, no migrations — connection only. Advances EQS clauses: real-time latency baseline establishment, 100% auditable data pipeline foundation, Zero Trust orientation (fail-closed on missing secrets).
directives.rationale: 19 consecutive failures share a common root cause pattern: directives attempt too much (schema + ORM + endpoints + dashboard) in one pass. directives.title: Core RFQ schema migration with Drizzle and seed script
directives.directive: Depends on AUTO-024 health check returning 200. Using Drizzle ORM, define the core schema in `lib/db/schema.ts` with three tables only: `rfqs` (id uuid PK, title text NOT NULL, status enum['draft','pending','awarded','closed'], created_at timestamptz default now(), updated_at timestamptz default now()), `rfq_line_items` (id uuid PK, rfq_id uuid FK->rfqs.id ON DELETE CASCADE, description text, quantity numeric(12,4), unit_price numeric(12,4)), and `audit_log` (id uuid PK, table_name text, record_id uuid, action text, changed_by text, changed_at timestamptz default now(), payload jsonb). Generate the migration file via `drizzle-kit generate` into `drizzle/migrations/`. Create `scripts/seed.ts` that inserts 3 sample RFQs with 2 line items each. Add `db:migrate` and `db:seed` npm scripts. Do NOT drop or rename any existing columns. Advances EQS clauses: financial accuracy ±0.1% (numeric precision on prices), 100% auditable data (audit_log table), immutable audit log orientation.
directives.rationale: Every previous schema attempt (AUTO-012, AUTO-015, AUTO-018, AUTO-021) bundled connection setup with schema creation, causing cascading failures. With AUTO-024 isolating the connection layer, this directive can assume a working client and focus solely on schema definition and migration — matching the Week 1 roadmap priority for Neon DB migration with the smallest safe scope increment.

directives.title: RFQ list API endpoint with Neon DB query layer
directives.directive: Create a single, minimal, verifiably working GET /api/rfq endpoint that: (1) imports the Drizzle client established in AUTO-025, (2) executes a SELECT on the rfq table with limit/offset pagination params, (3) returns JSON { data: RFQ[], total: number, page: number } with proper HTTP status codes, (4) includes a /api/health/db endpoint that returns { connected: boolean, latency_ms: number } by running a SELECT 1 query and measuring round-trip time. No authentication required on these endpoints for this directive — that is handled by middleware.ts. Constraints: response must complete in <500ms on cold start; use try/catch with structured error logging compatible with the logging infrastructure from AUTO-014; do not introduce new dependencies beyond what AUTO-025 already installed. Advances EQS clauses: real-time latency <300ms, 100% auditable data pipeline.
directives.rationale: AUTO-025 completed the core RFQ schema and seed script, establishing a stable DB foundation. The pattern of 20 failed directives shows complexity and dependency drift as root causes — this directive is intentionally minimal and tightly scoped to a single working API layer. Without a functioning data endpoint, all dashboard and visualization work is blocked. The /api/health/db endpoint provides immediate observability to confirm Neon connectivity before further build steps proceed.

directives.title: Static RFQ dashboard page consuming /api/rfq endpoint

Performance constraint: Largest Contentful Paint must be measurable via next build output and page must not import any module >50kb uncompressed. Advances EQS clauses: Dashboard load <1.5s, C-suite clarity <5 seconds, Tableau-level visualization groundwork.
directives.rationale: AUTO-002, AUTO-010, AUTO-013, AUTO-017, AUTO-019 all failed attempting RFQ dashboard pages — analysis shows they attempted too much (live DB + auth + charts + animations) in one pass. This directive deliberately constrains scope to a server-rendered table consuming a known-good API, with an explicit test requirement to prevent silent failures. Building on AUTO-026's endpoint makes this directive's dependency chain concrete and short.

directives.title: Environment validation guard and CI pre-flight check script
directives.directive: Create scripts/preflight.ts that: (1) reads all required environment variables (DATABASE_URL, NEXTAUTH_SECRET, NEXTAUTH_URL, and any others declared in a new src/config/env.ts zod schema), (2) attempts a Neon DB ping via the Drizzle client, (3) exits with code 1 and a human-readable error message listing every missing or invalid variable if validation fails, (4) exits with code 0 and prints a summary table of all validated vars (values masked) on success. Add 'preflight': 'tsx scripts/preflight.ts' to package.json scripts. Add a Jest unit test (env-validation.test.ts) that asserts the zod schema rejects configs missing DATABASE_URL. This script must not touch .github/workflows/** — it is invoked manually or by Builder agents before running migrations. Advances EQS clauses: 100% auditable data, Zero Trust orientation (no silent misconfiguration), SOC 2 readiness.
directives.rationale: AUTO-021 and AUTO-023 both failed on environment configuration and database connection validation. The 20 cumulative failures strongly suggest environment misconfiguration is silently breaking Builder agent runs — directives start executing against missing or wrong DATABASE_URL values and fail mid-run with cryptic errors. A reusable, testable preflight script eliminates this failure mode for all future directives and is a one-time investment that will unblock the entire pipeline.

directives.title: Immutable audit log table and POST /api/audit endpoint
directives.directive: Add an audit_logs table to the existing Drizzle schema (do NOT drop or rename any existing tables or columns — additive migration only). Schema: id (uuid pk), entity_type (varchar), entity_id (varchar), action (varchar: created|updated|viewed|status_changed), actor_id (varchar, nullable for system), payload (jsonb), created_at (timestamptz default now()). Set the table as INSERT-only at the application layer: the API route POST /api/audit must accept {entity_type, entity_id, action, actor_id?, payload} and write a record — no UPDATE or DELETE handlers may be implemented. Run the migration via Drizzle push. Wire the RFQ list endpoint (AUTO-026) and RFQ detail endpoint (AUTO-029) to emit a 'viewed' audit event on each successful response. This directly advances EQS clause: 100% auditable data, immutable audit logs (Week 3 audit trail wiring).
directives.rationale: Audit trail wiring is a Week-3 roadmap priority and an explicit EQS hard requirement. Introducing the audit_logs table now as an additive migration while the schema surface is small reduces future rework. The INSERT-only constraint at the application layer establishes the immutability pattern before more entities are wired in.

directives.title: Dashboard performance instrumentation and budget assertion
directives.directive: Implement a lightweight server-timing and performance budget instrumentation layer. Instrument /api/rfq and /api/rfq/[id] with this utility, measuring DB query duration. Add a /api/health/perf endpoint that returns a JSON report: {dashboard_ttfb_budget_ms: 1500, ai_inference_budget_ms: 2000, realtime_budget_ms: 300, db_query_last_ms: number, status: 'ok'|'degraded'}. In the CI pre-flight script (AUTO-028), add a step that calls /api/health/perf and fails the build if db_query_last_ms exceeds 500ms (a conservative sub-budget gate). This advances EQS Dashboard Performance clause (< 1.5s load) and provides the observability baseline required before Week-3 performance hardening.
directives.rationale: AUTO-004, AUTO-011, and AUTO-019 all failed attempting large-scope performance work. This directive takes a minimal, additive approach: a single utility file, two instrumented endpoints, and one health check — no bundler config changes, no webpack plugins. It establishes the measurement infrastructure that makes Week-3 performance optimization tractable and gives the CI pipeline an objective quality gate.

directives.title: Dashboard performance budget assertion and instrumentation
directives.directive: Implement end-to-end dashboard performance instrumentation and budget enforcement. (1) Add a server-side timing header (Server-Timing) to GET /api/rfq capturing DB query duration. (2) In the RFQ dashboard page (/app/dashboard or equivalent), instrument client-side navigation timing using the Performance API and log time-to-interactive to console in development. (3) Create a lightweight /api/health/perf endpoint that executes a representative DB query (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM rfqs) and returns { db_ms, status: 'ok'|'degraded' } where degraded triggers when db_ms > 200. (4) Add a Jest/Vitest integration test at tests/perf/dashboard-budget.test.ts that calls /api/rfq with a seeded DB and asserts response time < 500ms (synthetic budget with headroom under the 1.5s EQS target). Do NOT modify middleware.ts or any auth routes. All timing values must be logged to the existing audit_logs infrastructure via a non-blocking fire-and-forget pattern. directives.rationale: AUTO-031 (performance instrumentation) failed and AUTO-033 (audit log) is now complete, providing the prerequisite infrastructure. directives.title: FS-VIZ-MOB-003: Mobile Chart Data Density & Adaptive Tick Reduction
directives.directive: DIRECTIVE: Implement adaptive data density management for all charts across the 14 FlowSeer W251 BOP dashboard pages on mobile viewports (< 768px). The current chart implementation renders all data ticks and data points at full desktop density, causing overlapping labels and illegible axes on mobile. Required: (1) XAxis must automatically reduce tick count to a maximum of 5 ticks on viewports < 768px using Recharts `ticks` prop computed from the data array length and viewport width. (2) Line and area charts must reduce rendered data points to a maximum of 50 visible points on mobile via a `downsampleData(data, maxPoints)` utility (LTTB algorithm or uniform stride — no new deps). (3) Bar charts with > 10 bars must switch to a horizontally scrollable container on mobile (overflow-x: scroll, snap-type: x mandatory, snap-align: start per bar group) rather than compressing bars below 20px width. (4) All label truncation must use CSS `text-overflow: ellipsis` with a `title` attribute fallback for full value. (5) The downsample utility must preserve data min/max/last values for financial accuracy per EQS ±0.1% tolerance. CONTEXT: Next.js 14, Tailwind, TypeScript, Vercel. EQS v1.0 enforced. FS-VIZ-MOB-001 is a prerequisite. Scope limited to data density and axis rendering of visualization components — no layout, sidebar, grid, table, or gesture changes.

directives.title: FS-VIZ-MOB-002 — Mobile Chart Accessibility: ARIA Labels, Keyboard Nav & Screen Reader Support for W251 BOP Visualizatio
directives.directive: DIRECTIVE: Following FS-VIZ-MOB-001 (mobile responsive charts), add full accessibility support to all chart components on the W251 BOP dashboard. Every Recharts chart must have: (1) a wrapping <figure> with aria-label describing the chart title and data range; (2) a visually-hidden <figcaption> with a plain-language data summary updated dynamically when chart data changes; (3) all interactive chart elements (tooltip triggers, legend items) must be keyboard-focusable with visible focus rings meeting WCAG 2.1 AA 3:1 contrast ratio; (4) Recharts SVG must have role='img' and aria-label; (5) color encoding must not be the sole differentiator — patterns or shapes must supplement color for line/area charts. CONTEXT: Next.js 14, Tailwind, TypeScript, Vercel. EQS v1.0 enforced. This covers ONLY chart accessibility — no layout, auth, or data changes. Builds on ChartWrapper.tsx and BaseChart.tsx introduced in FS-VIZ-MOB-001.

directives.title: FS-VIZ-MOB-003 — Mobile Chart Interaction: Pinch-Zoom, Swipe-to-Pan & Touch Gesture Handling for W251 BOP Time-Series Ch
directives.directive: DIRECTIVE: Following FS-VIZ-MOB-001 (mobile responsive charts), add touch gesture support to time-series and trend charts on the W251 BOP dashboard. Requirements: (1) pinch-to-zoom on time-series charts adjusts the visible X-axis date range (min zoom = 1 day, max = full dataset range); (2) horizontal swipe-to-pan shifts the visible window without triggering page scroll; (3) double-tap resets zoom to full range; (4) all gestures must have visual affordance (zoom level indicator, pan handle icons); (5) gesture state must be managed locally in a useChartGestures hook — no global state changes; (6) gestures must not interfere with page scroll on the vertical axis. CONTEXT: Next.js 14, Tailwind, TypeScript, Vercel. Recharts only — no additional charting libraries. EQS v1.0 enforced. Builds on ChartWrapper.tsx and BaseChart.tsx from FS-VIZ-MOB-001. Covers ONLY gesture interaction — no layout, accessibility, or data changes.

For each such page file: (1) replace fixed width props with ResponsiveContainer wrappers, (2) apply useChartDimensions hook for tick interval and margin logic, (3) ensure tooltip overflow fix (allowEscapeViewBox, maxWidth: 90vw wrapperStyle) is applied. directives.title: FLOWSEER-RESP-002c: Automated Visual Regression Suite for Chart Responsiveness
directives.directive: After FLOWSEER-RESP-002 and FLOWSEER-RESP-002b land, create a Playwright visual regression test suite that: (1) captures screenshots of all 14 dashboard pages at 393x852 (iPhone 14 Pro), 768x1024 (iPad), and 1440x900 (desktop) viewports, (2) asserts no horizontal overflow (scrollWidth <= innerWidth) on every page, (3) simulates a tap on the first data point of each chart type (line, bar, scatter, heatmap) and asserts the tooltip bounding rect is within viewport bounds, (4) asserts chart SVG render completes within 1500ms using Performance API marks. Test files must live under tests/responsive/charts/. Maximum 5 files created. One concern: test suite only, no production code changes. EQS v1.0 pass rate = 100% required.

For each such instance: (1) replace fixed width props with ResponsiveChartWrapper from components/charts/ResponsiveChartWrapper.tsx, (2) apply the same calcTickInterval and clampTooltipPosition utilities, (3) reduce mobile margins, (4) ensure tooltip viewport clamping. directives.directive: After FLOWSEER-RESP-002 and FLOWSEER-RESP-002b/c land, audit all chart components for mobile accessibility: (1) ensure ARIA labels and roles are set on SVG/canvas elements, (2) verify keyboard navigation does not break after ResponsiveContainer wrapping, (3) add touch swipe support for paginated charts on mobile (e.g., time-series charts with dense data points), (4) verify VoiceOver/TalkBack can announce chart data summaries. directives.title: FLOWSEER-RESP-003-B: Audit and Replace Custom Floating Dropdown Components with Native-Compatible Implementations
directives.directive: After FLOWSEER-RESP-003 lands, audit all custom dropdown/combobox components in the W251 BOP platform codebase (components/ui/, any portal-based floating dropdowns using Radix Popper, Headless UI Listbox, or custom z-index overlays) that bypass the globals.css select normalization applied in RESP-003. (2) Document all components audited in a COMPONENTS_AUDIT.md file at repo root. (3) Ensure no custom floating dropdown has a z-index > 40 on mobile that would overlap the sidebar drawer. EQS constraint: zero custom floating dropdowns violating 44px targets on mobile after this directive lands. Maximum 5 files. One concern: dropdown/combobox mobile compliance only. No schema changes.

directives.title: FS-SCHEMA-003: access_denied_audit Table DDL and Migration
directives.directive: Create a standalone schema directive for the access_denied_audit table required by the FS-AUTH-002 authorization middleware. Scope: add the following to lib/db/schema.ts — export const accessDeniedAudit table definition with columns: id (uuid, primary key, defaultRandom), userId (text, not null), role (text, not null), path (text, not null), method (text, not null), ip (text), reason (text), deniedAt (timestamp with timezone, defaultNow, not null). Generate the corresponding Drizzle (or chosen ORM) migration file. No application logic changes. EQS v1.0 audit requirements apply: table must be append-only with no DELETE or UPDATE grants to the application role. Include a DB index on (userId, deniedAt) for audit query performance. This directive is schema-only — no middleware, no UI, no API route changes.

directives.title: FS-AUTH-003: /api/internal/audit Endpoint for Access Denial Log Ingestion
directives.directive: Implement the internal API route consumed by lib/auth/authorization-logger.ts (introduced in FS-AUTH-002). Scope: create app/api/internal/audit/route.ts (Next.js 14 App Router). This POST endpoint receives AuthDenialPayload { userId, role, path, method, ip, reason } and writes a row to the access_denied_audit table (defined in FS-SCHEMA-003). The route must: (1) verify the request carries a shared secret header X-FlowSeer-Internal-Token matching an env var INTERNAL_AUDIT_SECRET to prevent public access; (2) validate the payload with zod; (3) insert via the existing db client in lib/db; (4) respond 204 on success, 400 on validation failure, 401 on missing/invalid secret. No authentication middleware (this route is internal service-to-service). EQS v1.0: p99 response < 50ms, immutable insert only (no update/delete), full TypeScript strict mode. Depends on FS-SCHEMA-003 completing first.

directives.title: FS-SCHEMA-003: access_denied_audit Table Schema Migration
directives.directive: Using the FlowSeer W251 BOP platform schema conventions established in FS-SCHEMA-001 (lib/db/schema.ts, Drizzle ORM, PostgreSQL), add a new access_denied_audit table to capture authorization denial events emitted by the FS-AUTH-002 middleware. This is a schema-only directive. Columns required: id (uuid, primary key, default gen_random_uuid()), user_id (text, nullable — anonymous requests have no userId), role (UserRole enum, nullable), pathname (text, not null), method (text, not null), request_id (text, not null — correlates with the requestId generated in middleware), denied_at (timestamptz, not null, default now()), ip_address (text, nullable), user_agent (text, nullable). Table must be append-only by convention (no update/delete grants for the app role). Add the table definition to lib/db/schema.ts, generate and apply the Drizzle migration, and create the /api/internal/audit-denial POST endpoint stub (app/api/internal/audit-denial/route.ts) that validates the inbound payload with zod, inserts a row, and returns 202. The endpoint must be protected so only internal service calls (shared secret header or Vercel internal network) can invoke it — not exposed to end users. EQS v1.0 applies: insert latency < 50ms p99, immutable audit semantics, data lineage comment on table. No UI changes. No auth logic changes.

directives.title: FS-AUTH-004: Authorization Denial Audit Dashboard UI
directives.directive: Using the FlowSeer W251 BOP platform (Next.js 14, Tailwind, TypeScript), implement a read-only admin dashboard page at /dashboard/admin/access-audit that displays records from the access_denied_audit table (defined in FS-SCHEMA-003). Scope: server component page that queries access_denied_audit via Drizzle, renders a paginated table (50 rows/page) showing denied_at, user_id, role, pathname, method, request_id, ip_address columns. Filter controls: date range picker, role multi-select, pathname prefix text input. Accessible only to admin role (enforced by FS-AUTH-002 middleware). EQS v1.0 applies: page load < 1.5s, C-suite clarity in < 5 seconds, Tableau-level data table with sortable columns. No schema changes — consume access_denied_audit table from lib/db/schema.ts only.

directives.title: Fix residual audit findings from AUTO-050
directives.directive:
AUTO-050 ("Route Authorization Middleware for User Roles") shipped via the conditional-ship valve at 78/100 with zero CRITICAL findings. Resolve every residual audit finding below in the files committed by that directive. Do not change unrelated files, do not regress passing acceptance criteria.

RESIDUAL FINDINGS:
1. [HIGH] middleware.ts: The middleware awaits auditDenial() calls, which violates the spec requirement that auditDenial() must NEVER be awaited on the middleware critical path. The spec explicitly states 'auditDenial() must NEVER be awaited on the middleware critical path — use waitUntil or fire-and-forget keepalive fetch'. Currently, the middleware uses 'await auditDenial(...)' which blocks the response until the fetch completes. FIX: Remove the 'await' keyword before auditDenial() calls. The function is already fire-and-forget with .catch() error handling. Use request.waitUntil() from @vercel/functions if available, or simply call auditDenial() without await.
2. [HIGH] lib/auth/session.ts: The session.ts file contains a mock user database with hardcoded credentials and no actual auth providers configured. This is not a production-ready implementation and the spec requires the JWT encode callback to embed the role claim. The file has empty providers array which will cause next-auth to fail at runtime. FIX: Remove the mock user database. Implement proper auth providers (e.g., Credentials, OAuth) as appropriate for the application. Ensure the jwt() callback properly extracts the role from the authenticated user object.
3. [MEDIUM] lib/auth/route-permissions.ts: The ROUTE_RULES has a catch-all pattern /^\/api\/.*/ that matches ALL API routes including /api/internal/audit-denial which is the audit endpoint itself. This could cause the middleware to block the audit denial endpoint or create a circular dependency where the audit endpoint triggers another permission check. FIX: Add an explicit exclusion for /api/internal/* routes in the middleware skip logic or add a specific rule that allows all roles on /api/internal/* routes. Alternatively, move the catch-all API rule to only match routes that need protection.
4. [MEDIUM] middleware.ts: The middleware returns 401 for unauthenticated API requests instead of 403 as specified in the acceptance criteria. The spec says 'returns 403 for unauthorized access' and the acceptance criteria specifically mention 403 responses. While 401 is technically more correct for 'unauthenticated' vs 'unauthorized', the spec consistently requires 403. FIX: Change the 401 status to 403 for unauthenticated API requests to match the spec requirements, or add a clear distinction between 401 (not authenticated) and 403 (not authorized) as documented.
5. [MEDIUM] lib/auth/__tests__/middleware.test.ts: The test suite only tests the checkPermission pure function and auditDenial function in isolation. There are no integration tests that test the full middleware handler with mocked JWT tokens, which means acceptance criteria 3, 4, 5, and 6 cannot be fully verified through tests. FIX: Add integration tests that mock the NextRequest, getToken, and NextResponse to test the full middleware handler flow, verifying actual HTTP response status codes for each role/route/method combination.
6. [LOW] lib/auth/audit-denial.ts: The auditDenial function uses a relative URL '/api/internal/audit-denial' which may not resolve correctly in all environments (e.g., when middleware runs on edge, the base URL might differ). FIX: Construct the full URL using request.nextUrl.origin or an environment variable for the API base URL to ensure correct resolution in all environments.
directives.rationale: Auto-generated debt repayment for conditional-ship of AUTO-050

directives.title: AUTH-METRICS-002: Real-Time Denial Alert Streaming via SSE
directives.directive: Extend the authorization denial metrics layer (built in AUTH-METRICS-001) to push real-time denial spike alerts to the C-suite dashboard tile. Implement a Server-Sent Events endpoint at GET /api/metrics/auth-denials/stream that tails authorization_audit_log using Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY (trigger on INSERT where outcome='deny'). The SSE stream must emit an event within 300ms of a new denial row being committed (EQS real-time latency budget). On the client, AuthDenialTile's KPI counters must update without full page reload. Alert threshold: if denial count in any rolling 5-minute window exceeds 10, emit a 'spike' event that renders a red banner ('Unusual denial spike detected — {count} denials in 5 min') on the tile. All streamed data must reference authorization_audit_log row IDs for full auditability. No sampling. Depends on AUTH-METRICS-001 being merged.

directives.title: AUTH-METRICS-003: Denial Metrics CSV Export + Audit Trail Download
directives.directive: Add a CSV export capability to the authorization denial metrics tile (built in AUTH-METRICS-001). Implement a GET /api/metrics/auth-denials/export endpoint that streams a CSV of all raw authorization_audit_log rows (outcome='deny', last 24h by default, configurable via ?hours=N up to 168) with columns: id, user_id, role, action, resource, rfq_id, created_at. The export must include a checksum footer row (SHA-256 of all data rows) to enable offline reconciliation. Endpoint must require METRICS_READ_KEY header (HTTP 401 otherwise). On the AuthDenialTile, add a small 'Export CSV' button (visible only to users with role='c_suite' or 'auditor' — check via existing session/auth pattern). Export must complete within 2s for up to 10,000 rows. Depends on AUTH-METRICS-001 being merged.

directives.title: FS-RESP-004: Responsive Audit — Remaining Dashboard Pages (settings, notifications, reports, analytics-detail, user-prof

directives.title: FS-RESP-SHARED-001: Harden Shared KpiGrid.tsx and DataTable.tsx with Canonical Mobile-Responsive Defaults
directives.directive: After FS-RESP-003 and FS-RESP-004 complete all per-page patches, consolidate the responsive rules into the shared components so future pages are compliant by default.
